input.topic: End-of-day update on a day where a ticket slipped. Name the slip, reassure on launch date (buffer is there for this).
input.scenario: Sent at 5pm ET by the build agent when planned-vs-shipped delta exceeds one ticket. Customer trust hinges on Jack naming the miss without spin.
input.voice: Operator. Honest about the slip. No spin. No 'unfortunately'. Signed '— Jack / Day14'.

variants.subject: Day {{n}} — slower than I wanted
variants.rationale: Keep the current line. First-person admission earns trust precisely because it skips the corporate hedge.

variants.subject: Day {{n}} — slipped a ticket, still on track
variants.rationale: Names the slip AND the reassurance in one breath. The customer gets the full update before they open.

variants.subject: Day {{n}} — burning some of the buffer today
variants.rationale: Reframes the slip as a planned-for cost, which it is. Reinforces that the SOW has a buffer and Jack knows where he stands against it.

constraints: Name the slip; don't hide it.
